Best Luxury Hotels in Da Nang

Da Nang is Southeast Asia's most undervalued luxury destination — 25+ verified five-star resorts, from the InterContinental's clifftop jungle villas to private-pool beachfront estates along Non Nuoc. World-class spa retreats, Michelin-starred dining, and beachfront five-stars at 40–60% below Phuket prices. See also our complete neighbourhood guide and beach hotel guide.

Overview

Why Da Nang for Luxury Travel

Da Nang's luxury hotels span three distinct settings: clifftop jungle, open beachfront, and city river views

Da Nang has quietly matured into one of the most compelling luxury destinations in Southeast Asia — and it remains significantly underpriced relative to its peers. What Phuket charges $400–$600 for, Da Nang delivers at $200–$350. What the Maldives charges $1,000+, Son Tra Peninsula delivers with equivalent privacy and service for a third of the cost. The comparison holds across every segment of the luxury market.

The geography does the work. Three distinct resort corridors give luxury travellers a genuine choice of setting. Son Tra Peninsula — a protected nature reserve 20 minutes from the city — hosts the InterContinental Sun Peninsula and Naman Retreat in cliffside jungle and lagoon settings that feel genuinely remote. The Non Nuoc resort corridor 15 km south holds the Hyatt Regency and Furama in expansive beachfront compounds with wide, calm beaches well suited to families. My Khe Beach runs north through the city, placing the Sheraton Grand, Pullman, and Marriott within reach of Da Nang's restaurant scene while maintaining full resort infrastructure.

Across all three zones, the luxury tier now delivers private villas with plunge pools, Michelin-recognised dining, international spa brands, and private beach access. Da Nang's only MICHELIN Star restaurant — La Maison 1888 at the InterContinental — sits within a property that regularly appears on Condé Nast Traveler's Asia top-resort lists. The value argument is not a compromise: the properties here are world-class.

This guide covers 25+ verified luxury properties — only hotels averaging $200+ per night at mid-season rack rate. Browse by category below, or use the neighbourhood guide to find your ideal zone, or the beach hotels guide for beachfront-focused picks.

✦ The value argument

Da Nang luxury rates are 40–60% lower than comparable resorts in Phuket, Bali, or the Maldives. The InterContinental Sun Peninsula — ranked in Condé Nast Traveler's Top 10 Resorts in Asia — starts from $340/night. An equivalent property in the Maldives starts from $800. Private villa resorts like Naman Retreat and TIA Wellness start from $280/night for standalone villas with pools.

Interactive Map

Da Nang Luxury Hotels Map

All 25+ luxury resort locations across Da Nang's three resort corridors — Son Tra Peninsula, My Khe Beach, and Non Nuoc.

Son Tra Peninsula
Clifftop & Jungle Luxury

Home to the InterContinental Sun Peninsula and Naman Retreat. A UNESCO-recognised nature reserve 20 minutes from the city. Secluded private bays, jungle-canopy villas, and the only MICHELIN Star restaurant in Da Nang.

Non Nuoc Beach
Resort Corridor South

Da Nang's grandest resort strip — Hyatt Regency, Furama, TIA Wellness, and Marriott command wide, calm beachfront here. Best for families and multi-night stays; 15 km south of the city centre near Marble Mountains.

My Khe Beach
Central Beachfront Strip

The Sheraton Grand, Pullman, and Radisson Blu line the 33 km urban beach with full resort amenities within walking distance of Da Nang's restaurant and nightlife scene.

Han River / City
Urban Luxury Hotels

The Novotel Premier, Hilton Da Nang, and Crowne Plaza offer five-star city stays with Dragon Bridge views. Ideal for business travellers or those combining Da Nang with nearby Hoi An day trips.

Ultra Luxury · Son Tra & Non Nuoc

Ultra Luxury Resorts — Iconic Properties

Da Nang's four flagship ultra-luxury properties rank among the finest resorts in Southeast Asia. Private bays, jungle hillsides, designer villas, and spa programmes that define the category for Vietnam.

Editor's Choice · Ultra Luxury

InterContinental Sun Peninsula Resort

The resort's cascading architecture follows the jungle hillside, ensuring every room opens onto canopy or sea views

The most-awarded property in Central Vietnam and — on most metrics — the finest resort in the country. Set within the protected Son Tra Nature Reserve on a forested hillside above a private bay, the InterContinental was designed by architect Bill Bensley with a specific brief: build something spectacular without destroying the environment it sits in. The result is a cascading series of structures that follow the hillside contour, with rooms and villas that open onto jungle canopy rather than car parks.

La Maison 1888, the on-site restaurant, holds Da Nang's only MICHELIN Star and is helmed by a French executive chef working with Central Vietnamese ingredients. The private bay below the resort has calm water sheltered from the South China Sea swell, making it genuinely swimmable year-round — a significant advantage during the October–November monsoon period when My Khe Beach is effectively closed.

⭐ Top Luxury Pick
InterContinental Sun Peninsula Resort
📍 Son Tra Peninsula · Nature Reserve · 20 min from city
Private beachMICHELIN Star diningPrivate pool villasJungle hillside settingButler service

Most-awarded resort in Central Vietnam. Jungle-to-sea architecture, MICHELIN Star dining at La Maison 1888, private bay with year-round swimmable water, and private pool villas designed by Bill Bensley. Included city transfers and full butler service. Best for milestone trips and honeymooners.

Best for: honeymooners, milestone trips, travellers who won't want to leave the property.

From $380/night
View on Booking.com
Ultra Luxury · Wellness

TIA Wellness Resort

The leading wellness resort in Da Nang and one of the finest spa retreats in Southeast Asia. TIA sits on a private stretch of Non Nuoc Beach with a programme of spa treatments, detox meals, yoga pavilions, and hydrotherapy pools built around genuine wellbeing outcomes rather than holiday-spa filler. All-inclusive wellness packages make pricing straightforward; guests typically book four or five nights to fully engage the programme.

The residential-style villas have private garden terraces and plunge pools. Beach access is direct and the stretch of sand here is wider and quieter than My Khe. This is a resort you book specifically for the programme — the destination itself would be compelling at twice the price.

Ultra Luxury
TIA Wellness Resort
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · 15 km south of city
Spa retreatPrivate villasPrivate beachPlunge poolsWellness programme

Da Nang's premier wellness destination. Private beach villas with plunge pools, a 2,000 sqm spa, holistic wellness packages, and yoga pavilions. All-inclusive wellbeing programmes designed for 4–7 night stays. Quieter Non Nuoc beachfront with direct sand access.

Best for: wellness travellers, couples seeking a spa-focused retreat, burnout recovery stays.

From $320/night
View on Booking.com
Ultra Luxury · Villas

Naman Retreat

Naman Retreat is what happens when a resort takes the word "retreat" at face value. The property occupies a private beachfront site on the Non Nuoc strip with a collection of free-standing villas arranged around freshwater lagoon pools and tropical gardens. Architecture by Vo Trong Nghia — one of Vietnam's most acclaimed contemporary architects — makes it visually distinctive among Da Nang's resort landscape.

The spa uses Vietnamese botanical ingredients and the dining programme — across three restaurants — is anchored by locally sourced Central Vietnamese cuisine treated with fine-dining technique. The lagoon pools are genuinely stunning. This is a property where the design is the experience.

Ultra Luxury
Naman Retreat
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · Lagoon villas · 15 km south
Private beachLagoon pool villasAward-winning designSpa3 restaurants

Award-winning beachfront villa resort designed by Vo Trong Nghia. Standalone pool villas, freshwater lagoon, and Vietnamese botanical spa. Three restaurants covering Central Vietnamese, international, and beach-grill formats. Private beach access on quiet Non Nuoc stretch.

Best for: design-conscious travellers, honeymoons, anyone prioritising privacy and aesthetics.

From $230/night
View on Booking.com
Luxury Beachfront Resorts

Sheraton Grand Danang Resort

Sheraton Grand Danang — prime position on My Khe Beach with direct sand access, beach club, and three pools

Five-Star
Sheraton Grand Danang Resort
📍 My Khe Beach · central strip · near An Thuong village
Private beachBeach club3 poolsPool villasOceanfront suites

The best five-star on My Khe for travellers who want beach luxury with genuine city access. Three pools (one adults-only), a proper beach club with sun-lounger service, daily seafood from the morning market, and pool villas with direct beach gate access. An Thuong's restaurant scene is within a 5-minute walk.

Best for: couples and solo travellers wanting beach luxury without the isolation of Son Tra.

From $210/night
View on Booking.com
Ultra Luxury · Best for Families & Space

Hyatt Regency Da Nang Resort & Spa

Hyatt Regency Da Nang — six hectares on Non Nuoc Beach, pools terracing down to privately maintained sand

Ultra Luxury
Hyatt Regency Da Nang Resort & Spa
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · 6-hectare compound · 15 km south of city
Private beach4 poolsKids clubPool villasSpa retreat

Six hectares, four terraced pools descending to the sand, a serious kids' programme, and room categories from garden standards to private pool villas. The Non Nuoc beach here is wider and calmer than My Khe — genuinely better for families with young children. Marble Mountains are a 5-minute drive; Hoi An 35 minutes south.

Best for: families and couples on longer stays who want resort scale on a quieter beach.

From $180/night
View on Booking.com
Best Value Five-Star · Beachfront

Pullman Da Nang Beach Resort

Pullman Da Nang — central My Khe Beach, beachside restaurant sourcing daily from the An Thuong morning market

Five-Star
Pullman Da Nang Beach Resort
📍 My Khe Beach · central strip · walkable to An Thuong
Private beachBeachside diningOceanview poolSpa

A well-positioned five-star that tracks lower than the Sheraton Grand without a significant quality drop. Multiple renovations have kept rooms contemporary. The ground-floor beachside restaurant operates as a neighbourhood destination — seafood sourced from the morning market 200 metres away. Rates are more stable than big-brand neighbours, making it the best-value five-star on the beach strip.

Best for: five-star experience without the premium pricing of the Sheraton. Strong solo and couple choice.

From $165/night
View on Booking.com
Luxury Beachfront Resort

Furama Resort Danang

One of Da Nang's original international luxury resorts and still one of the most complete. Furama occupies a sweeping beachfront position on Non Nuoc Beach with a compound that feels more self-contained village than hotel: multiple restaurants, a MICHELIN-Selected spa, tennis courts, and a beach club with full sun-lounger service. The longer-established landscaping gives Furama a maturity and shade quality that newer builds lack.

Five-Star
Furama Resort Danang
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · Hoa Hai Ward · 15 km south
Private beachBeach clubAward-winning spaMultiple restaurantsTennis courts

Da Nang's original international luxury resort — established tropical landscaping, three pools, MICHELIN-Selected spa, and a private stretch of Non Nuoc Beach. Multiple restaurants across Vietnamese, international, and Japanese formats. Full resort infrastructure makes it genuinely self-contained for multi-night stays.

Best for: couples and families wanting an established resort with mature grounds and full amenities.

From $175/night
View on Booking.com
Luxury Beachfront Resort

Da Nang Marriott Resort & Spa

The Marriott's Non Nuoc Beach position gives it one of the widest beachfronts in the resort corridor. The property's standout feature is its Quan Spa — a 2,000 sqm facility drawing on Vietnamese traditional therapies — which makes it worth considering even as a day visitor from a neighbouring property. Room tiers run from garden-view doubles to oceanfront suites and private pool villas. The signature restaurant, Hai, focuses on Central Vietnamese cuisine with international interpretation.

Five-Star
Da Nang Marriott Resort & Spa
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · Marble Mountains district · 16 km south
Private beachQuan SpaPool villasOceanfront suitesKids pool

International-standard Marriott resort on wide Non Nuoc beachfront. The 2,000 sqm Quan Spa is one of Da Nang's finest. Oceanfront suites, private pool villas, and a dedicated kids' pool. Central Vietnamese fine dining at Hai restaurant. Marble Mountains and Hoi An both within easy reach.

Best for: Marriott Bonvoy members, spa-focused couples, and families wanting a quiet resort stretch.

From $240/night
View on Booking.com
Luxury Beachfront Resort

Premier Village Danang Resort

Premier Village occupies a rare peninsula position at the northern tip of My Khe Beach — one of the few resorts in Da Nang where guests can access both the East Sea on one side and a sheltered lagoon on the other from within the same property. The villa format (all accommodation is standalone villas rather than hotel rooms) gives it a more residential feel than its neighbours, and the pools within each villa allow privacy that shared hotel pool infrastructure cannot replicate.

Five-Star
Premier Village Danang Resort
📍 My Khe Beach peninsula · Son Tra district · city fringe
Peninsula locationPrivate pool villasDual sea accessPrivate beach

All-villa luxury resort on Da Nang's only peninsula site — sea on both sides. Freestanding pool villas from two to five bedrooms make it the city's best option for groups and families wanting privacy at the luxury tier. Direct beach access and a dedicated kids club. City centre 10 minutes north by Grab.

Best for: families, groups, and villa-style stays combining city access with private resort feel.

From $390/night
View on Booking.com
Villa Resorts

Luxury Villa Resorts — Private Pool & Villas

Da Nang's villa resort category delivers the region's most private luxury experiences — freestanding residences with plunge pools, secluded garden settings, and boutique-scale service. These properties suit honeymooners, anniversary travellers, and anyone who finds hotel corridors antithetical to relaxation.

Villa Resort
The Ocean Villas
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · private villa compound
Private pool villasBeach accessGarden settingSpa services

A collection of standalone villas on Non Nuoc Beach, each with a private swimming pool, enclosed garden, and direct beach access. The boutique scale — just 45 villas — delivers a level of quiet and personalised service that larger resorts structurally cannot replicate. Ideal for guests seeking pool-villa privacy at rates significantly below comparable Bali properties.

Best for: honeymoons, privacy-focused couples, longer stays of 5+ nights.

From $220/night
View on Booking.com
Villa Resort
Fusion Resort & Villas Danang
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · all-inclusive spa villas
All-spa-inclusivePrivate pool villasPrivate beachDaily spa treatments

Fusion's defining offer: all spa treatments are included in the room rate — a policy that makes it exceptional value for spa-focused travellers. Every villa has a private pool. The resort's two daily spa treatments per person policy has built a devoted repeat-guest base. Beach access is direct; the Non Nuoc stretch here is quieter than My Khe. No strict meal times — a genuinely flexible luxury stay.

Best for: wellness couples, spa enthusiasts, and anyone who values all-inclusive simplicity at the luxury tier.

From $340/night
View on Booking.com
Villa Resort
Vinpearl Luxury Danang
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · Vinpearl resort complex
Private villasPrivate beachMultiple poolsKids activitiesSpa

Vietnam's leading domestic luxury brand delivers a full resort compound on Non Nuoc with private villas, direct beach access, multiple pools, and a comprehensive kids programme. The Vinpearl brand carries serious service infrastructure and the property's scale — over 100 villas — means facilities remain fully stocked regardless of occupancy.

Best for: families seeking villa-level privacy with resort-scale amenities and a trusted Vietnamese brand.

From $215/night
View on Booking.com
Villa Resort
Grandvrio Ocean Resort Danang
📍 Non Nuoc Beach · Japanese-managed resort
Private beachOcean villasJapanese service standardSpa

Japanese-managed luxury resort on Non Nuoc with a service ethos that reflects omotenashi principles — meticulous attention to detail and anticipatory service that consistently surprises Western guests. Ocean-view villas, private beach, and a spa programme that blends Vietnamese botanical treatments with Japanese wellness traditions.

Best for: travellers who prioritise service precision and guests familiar with Japanese hospitality standards.

From $180/night
View on Booking.com
Value Context

Da Nang vs. the Competition

The case for Da Nang luxury is fundamentally a value argument. Here's how five-star pricing in Da Nang compares to equivalent properties across Southeast Asia's luxury market.

Da Nang
$200–$600
Five-star range per night. InterContinental from $340, villa resorts from $210, city five-stars from $200.
Phuket
$350–$900
Comparable five-star resort quality. 40–60% premium over Da Nang at every tier.
Maldives
$700–$2,500+
Overwater bungalows and comparable privacy to Son Tra villas. 3–5x Da Nang rates.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Is the InterContinental Sun Peninsula worth the price?
For the right trip, yes. The InterContinental is genuinely world-class — it holds a place on Condé Nast Traveler's global top-hotel lists — and the on-site MICHELIN Star restaurant (La Maison 1888) makes it a destination in its own right. At $340+ per night it's not cheap in absolute terms, but relative to what $340 buys in the Maldives or Bali, it represents exceptional value. Best for honeymooners, milestone trips, and anyone who wants to spend several nights without needing to leave the property.
What's the best luxury hotel on the beach in Da Nang?
The Sheraton Grand Danang Resort on My Khe Beach is the best combination of five-star quality and direct beach access. Three pools, a proper beach club, pool villas with direct sand gates, and the An Thuong restaurant scene within walking distance. The Pullman Da Nang Beach Resort offers comparable quality at a lower price point. For the Non Nuoc resort strip, Hyatt Regency and Furama Resort both have excellent beachfronts.
Which luxury hotel is best for families in Da Nang?
The Hyatt Regency Da Nang Resort & Spa at Non Nuoc Beach is the best luxury option for families. Six hectares of resort, four terraced pools including dedicated children's areas, a serious kids' programme, and room categories with properly configured family layouts. The beach at Non Nuoc is calmer than My Khe, which matters significantly for families with young children. Premier Village Danang Resort — with large multi-bedroom villas — is another strong family option. See the full family hotel guide for more detail.
What is the best luxury spa resort in Da Nang?
TIA Wellness Resort is the standout for spa-focused stays — its all-inclusive wellness programme, 2,000 sqm spa, and dedicated detox cuisine make it the most comprehensive wellness property in the city. Fusion Resort & Villas includes daily spa treatments in the room rate for all guests, making it exceptional value for spa enthusiasts. Naman Retreat's Vietnamese botanical spa is also highly regarded for its design and treatment quality.
Is there a luxury hotel in Da Nang city centre?
Several. The Hilton Da Nang and Novotel Premier Han River both sit on the riverfront with Dragon Bridge views and rooftop pools. The Crowne Plaza sits at the city-beach intersection. All three are legitimate five-star city hotels within walking distance of Da Nang's restaurant and café scene. Beach access from each requires a 10–15 minute Grab ride.
How does Da Nang luxury compare to Phuket or Bali?
Da Nang's luxury resorts are 40–60% cheaper than comparable properties in Phuket and typically 50–70% cheaper than Bali at the same quality tier. The InterContinental Sun Peninsula, which appears on the same global top-resort lists as Amanpuri Phuket and Four Seasons Bali, costs around $340–$600 per night depending on season. Amanpuri starts from $1,200. The quality comparison holds up; the price difference is structural, not a reflection of inferior product.
What are the best luxury villas in Da Nang?
The best private pool villa options are: Naman Retreat (design-forward lagoon villas), TIA Wellness Resort (garden pool villas with wellness programme), Fusion Resort & Villas (all-spa-inclusive pool villas), Premier Village (multi-bedroom peninsula villas for groups), and The Ocean Villas (boutique-scale private pool villas on Non Nuoc). All include direct beach access.
RY
Ryan Yousefi
Founder & Editor
